up.method: average consumption = C_phenol/Δt (time to complete phenol depletion), Table 1

growth_records.cite: Szilveszter, Szabolcs et al. Kinetic characterization of a new phenol degrading Acinetobacter towneri strain isolated from landfill leachate treating bioreactor. World Journal of Microbiology & Biotechnology (2023). DOI:10.1007/s11274-022-03487-y. [PMC9842574]
growth_records.snippet: Acinetobacter sp. CFII-95 Average phenol consumption rate (mg/L/h): C_phenol=100 mg/L -> 12.5 (Table 1)
growth_records.notes: Table 1 screening: average phenol consumption (volumetric uptake) rate = C_phenol/Δt = 100 mg/L / time-to-complete-depletion = 12.5 mg/L/h, experimentally measured. No specific growth rate/biomass/yield reported for this strain at this condition, so qS(specific) not derivable. Species-level ID only for A. towneri strains (CFII-101) and Arthrobacter crystallopoietes (CFII-104, CFII-112); others reported as Acinetobacter sp. Same MP medium, 28 C, 150 rpm, pH not restated for assay (enrichment pH 8).

phenotypes.base_medium: Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it
